title: "How to launch wine/cedega in a seperate xserver"
date: 2007-05-03
forum: Gaming &amp; Leisure

### Post by noxxle on 2007-05-03

### Post by cogadh on 2007-05-03
No reconfiguring of you xorg.conf is required. See the 6th post in this thread: [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=423167](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=423167) for instructions on creating a script that will launch a game through Wine on a separate X session.

---

### Post by noxxle on 2007-05-03
I also tried this script:

#!/bin/sh

# Launch new X session on display 3
X :3 -ac & 

# Force the system to break for 2 seconds
sleep 2 

# Launch Cedega
DISPLAY=:3 /usr/bin/cedega

Which successfully launches cedega in its own xserver, but 3d acceleration doesnt work.... any ideas?

dobbbob@dobb:~$ Synaptics DeviceOn called
Could not init font path element /usr/X11R6/lib/X11/fonts/misc, removing from list!
Could not init font path element /usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic, removing from list!
Could not init font path element /usr/X11R6/lib/X11/fonts/Type1, removing from list!
Synaptics DeviceOff called
FreeFontPath: FPE "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc" refcount is 2, should be 1; fixing.

Well, that one's easy.  You need to make your wine command wine "this is my command.exe" since Program Files is two separate words and it doesn't know that otherwise.

As far as the other one goes...there's no reason why starting a separate server would break 3D acceleration.  Maybe there are some permissions issues.  We'd need to see the Xorg.3.log (I think that's what's generated) output.

### Post by cogadh on 2007-05-03
Change the command line to:
/home/dobbbob/.wine/drive_c/Program\Files/Shiny/Sacrifice/Sacrifice.exe

You need to place a backslash between "Program" and "Files" to represent the space. As for the 3D accelleration, I'd start by looking at your video card. I'm not too familiar with the Intel line of integrated graphics cards, but I'm sure if you searched for 3D accelleration and your card's name, you would find a how-to.

EDIT-
> **buttons said:**
> Oof.  Need coffee.  There's your 3D problem.  It doesn't appear to work at all.
What's weird is it is looking for a video card with PCI BusID 0:2:1, but the xorg.conf file has the video card listed as ID 0:2:0. I think that the xorg.conf is right but the new X session is looking at the wrong device for some reason.

### Post by cogadh on 2007-05-03
Try following the extra complicated instructions that kill your current X session and then launch the game in its own X session. Accelleration may be screwed because you are still running X on a different terminal.

EDIT - Stupid question: does the game run in the current X session at all? The script won't work if your game doesn't already work through Wine or Cedega first.

---

### Post by cogadh on 2007-05-03
Yes, it is very impractical, but it may explain what is going on. Using the plain script, your game is launched in a seperate terminal on a seperate X session. Your original X session does remain active, in fact you can switch back to it by pressing CTRL-ALT-F7 at any time. Chances are your video card/chipset's resources are being occupied by that X session so that when a new session is started, it can't get the necessary resources for accelleration to work in that session. Unfortunately, I don't know any way to get those resources away from your default session other than by killing it.

---

### Post by noxxle on 2007-05-03
> **cogadh said:**
> Yes, it is very impractical, but it may explain what is going on. Using the plain script, your game is launched in a seperate terminal on a seperate X session. Your original X session does remain active, in fact you can switch back to it by pressing CTRL-ALT-F7 at any time. Chances are your video card/chipset's resources are being occupied by that X session so that when a new session is started, it can't get the necessary resources for accelleration to work in that session. Unfortunately, I don't know any way to get those resources away from your default session other than by killing it.

My card does support dual display. Is it possible to set my xorg up to use two displays, and just launch the seperate x session on the second one?

---

### Post by buttons on 2007-05-03
I'd just like to point out that *just* a \ between Program and Files is insufficient.  The purpose of the \ is to escape the character that comes after it, in this case a space.

Soo..if you want to make a path without encapsulating it in quotes, you need to make it Program\ Files to inform your program there is a space there and you really want it to treat it as such.

Make a folder with a space and try it.

---

### Post by cogadh on 2007-05-04
> **noxxle said:**
> My card does support dual display. Is it possible to set my xorg up to use two displays, and just launch the seperate x session on the second one?
I don't believe so. X would be looking for a separate physical monitor for the second display, which you do not have. There may be a way to "trick" X into thinking you have two monitors, but I can't begin to think how you could do that. Somebody way smarter than me will have to figure that one out.

---
